name: set_non_present_forces_empty
code:
proof fn phi_4_set_non_present_forces_empty(pm: PageMap, index: usize, value: PageEntry)
    requires
        pm.wf(),
        0 <= index < 512,
        value.perm.present == false,
        value.is_empty(),
    ensures
        ({
            let new_view = pm@.update(index as int, value);
            new_view[index as int].addr == 0
            &&
            new_view[index as int].perm.ps == false
            &&
            new_view[index as int].perm.write == false
            &&
            new_view[index as int].perm.execute_disable == false
            &&
            new_view[index as int].perm.user == false
        }),
{
}
reason: set() requires non-present entries to be fully empty — this prevents storing non-present entries with non-zero addr or other permission bits, silently discarding information that could be useful for lazy allocation or dirty-bit tracking
